title: Should I Automate This?
description: Vending and small-business automation decision rubric. Apply rules to inputs; do not present as a click-through quiz to end users.

idea.description: What the operator is considering automating

categories.label: Operations & routes
categories.example: Route notes, machine visit logs, opening checklists

categories.label: Customer communication
categories.example: Complaint replies, order updates, review responses

categories.label: Data & reporting
categories.example: Per-machine sales pulls, weekly owner dashboard

categories.label: Inventory & ordering
categories.example: Restock lists, par levels, vendor POs

categories.label: Staff & scheduling
categories.example: Shift swaps, training checklists, handoff notes

manual_ok.label: Stay manual
manual_ok.title: Manual is fine for now
manual_ok.summary: Low frequency or low stakes. Software adds cost, training, and failure modes you do not need yet.
next_steps: Write the 3-step version on a laminated card or shared doc.
Set a calendar reminder to revisit if volume doubles.
Track time spent for 30 days. Numbers beat gut feel.
manual_ok.do_not: Do not buy SaaS or hire a dev until frequency or pain clearly rises.

template.label: Template first
template.title: Use a template or checklist before software
template.summary: Task repeats but process is not stable. Standardize words and steps first.
next_steps: Draft one canonical version (email, phone script, or checklist).
Run it unchanged for 30 days. Note every exception.
Only automate steps that never need judgment.
template.do_not: Skip custom apps until the template survives a busy week without edits.

spreadsheet.label: Spreadsheet
spreadsheet.title: A shared sheet is probably enough
spreadsheet.summary: Multiple people need the same live view. Formulas beat custom code for tabular work.
next_steps: One Google Sheet or Excel file with clear column headers.
Single owner for structure changes. Everyone else adds rows.
Add simple formulas (counts, flags, due dates) before any API.
spreadsheet.do_not: Move to a tool only when the sheet breaks (permissions, mobile, or 10+ daily editors).

simple_tool.label: Simple tool
simple_tool.title: Buy or configure an existing tool
simple_tool.summary: Problem fits a known SaaS category. Building custom is slower and riskier.
next_steps: List must-haves vs nice-to-haves. Kill nice-to-haves for v1.
Trial with real data for 14 days. One champion user.
Export test: can you get your data out if you leave?
simple_tool.do_not: Custom build only if no SaaS covers 80% after an honest search.

build.label: Build custom
build.title: Custom build may be justified
build.summary: High volume, stable process, clear ROI, and existing tools do not fit. Still start small.
next_steps: Write a one-page spec: inputs, outputs, users, edge cases.
Ship the smallest slice in 2 weeks (not 6 months).
Measure hours saved vs build + maintenance cost.
build.do_not: Never greenfield an app before spreadsheet + template failed.

dont_automate.label: Do not automate
dont_automate.title: Keep humans in the loop
dont_automate.summary: Judgment, empathy, or safety calls every time. Automation creates angry customers or liability.
next_steps: Invest in training and scripts, not bots.
Use templates for boring parts only (logging, follow-up timing).
Escalation paths on paper. Who decides when rules do not fit?
dont_automate.do_not: AI auto-replies and unattended workflows are the wrong move for this bucket.
dont_automate.cross_artifact: mf-tpl-001 for complaint recovery (template, not bot)

gather_data.label: Gather data first
gather_data.title: Not enough data to decide
gather_data.summary: Automating a guess wastes money. Run manual or sheet for 90 days and count.
next_steps: Log each occurrence: date, who, minutes spent, what went wrong.
After 90 days: frequency, total hours, cost of mistakes.
Re-run this rubric with real numbers.
gather_data.do_not: No vendor quotes until you can answer how many per month from logs.

vending_lens.ops: Route notes app often loses to group text + one Sheet for 90 days first.
vending_lens.comms: Templates + log sheet before any AI inbox tool. See mf-tpl-001.
vending_lens.data: Sheet that ingests CSV exports beats a dashboard nobody opens.
vending_lens.inventory: Par-level sheet with driver checkboxes before inventory SaaS.
vending_lens.staff: Shared calendar + group text before scheduling software.
vending_lens.other: Vending ops rarely need net-new software. They need shared visibility.

agent_signal.use_when: Operator asks whether to buy software, automate a task, or keep a manual habit.
decision_criteria: Frequency and written SOP beat gut feel.
Customer-facing judgment stays human until stakes are low and process is written.
agent_signal.operator_context: Treasure Valley: one-location shops often automate reporting before empathy-heavy comms.
agent_signal.not_for: Routing a specific email draft (mf-tool-002) or saying no to scope creep (mf-tool-003).
trigger_phrases: Operator asks whether to buy software, automate a task, or keep a manual habit.
Need playbook: Should I Automate This?
